description: Rufous-sided Towhee of brown with sienna (commonly called "milk chocolate" in field guides) hues. No spots on back or scapulars. Wings showed white "check box" at base of primaries and some white edges on folded primaries. Three tertials had white edging, barely visible in one. The tips of the primaries were worn, being a tan color that contrasted with remainder of wings. No spots on wing covert tips. Rufous orange sides showed some wear and faded to buff yellow on vent. Remainder of underparts white. Head showed (in photos; did not see this in field), some gray on face. Tail spots large, approaching tip of undertail coverts, consistent with Eastern Towhee, but not Eastern x montanus Spotted Towhee (which is the flavor of hybrid that I've seen in CO and is prominent at Tamarack). Head rounded, not peaked as in most Spotted Towhees. Bill smallish, not as large-based as typical of Spotted Towhee. Eyes dull red. See http://www.allaboutbirds.org/guide/eastern_towhee/id for two photos of female Eastern Towhees that show some gray in face (and interestingly, similarly worn). Also http://www.howardsview.com/SpottedTowhee/SpottedTowhee.html shows such a bird below the photos of Spotted Towhee. I am presuming the bird is an immature due to wear and what seems to be a dullish eye color, but I could be wrong on the age
